source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the physical architecture of a 1.8 m, 98 mm ID student solid-propellant sounding rocket: nose cone + recovery bay + airframe body tube + internal recovery bulkhead + motor mount tube + centering rings + fins + fin can + couplers + nozzle. This is the conventional assembly stackup for a KNSB-class single-stage amateur rocket. No rulebook citations are added beyond those present in the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the airframe and internal structure of a university-student rocket for a national student-rocket launch benchmark. Gross lift-off weight (GLOW) must be 9 kg with a tolerance of plus or minus 500 g (8.5 kg to 9.5 kg total). Apogee altitude is hard-capped at 400 m above launch rail height — exceeding 400 m is disqualification. Total propellant mass must not exceed 400 g. Static stability margin (CG-to-CP separation) must be between 1.0 and 2.5 calibers (one caliber equals one body-tube outside diameter). Ammonium perchlorate and potassium perchlorate oxidizers are explicitly prohibited per the rulebook safety rules; allowable propellants are typically KNSB (potassium nitrate / sorbitol) or commercially certified solid motors not on the banned list. Body tube ID is selected to accommodate the team's internal electronics bay and recovery system; this prompt targets 98 mm ID, total length approximately 1.8 m, and a phenolic or carbon-wrapped body tube. The airframe must survive (a) peak motor thrust phase with 12 g axial acceleration (equivalent quasi-static), (b) 8 g lateral gust, and (c) 10 g recovery deployment shock. Internal bulkheads must not deflect more than 1.5 mm under the 12 g axial load. The fin root attachment must survive a 75 N side force at the fin tip. A linear buckling eigenvalue on the body tube under 12 g axial must yield a load factor of at least 1.5. Primary verification is structural FEA (linear static + linear buckling) plus a stability-margin check via Barrowman / OpenRocket (non-FEA). Propellant mass and altitude cap are compliance checks tied to propellant data sheets and OpenRocket simulation output. Vibration, thermal, fluid (CFD), and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the airframe + internal bays, a mass budget, and a report listing GLOW in kg, propellant mass in g, stability caliber, body tube buckling load factor at 12 g, and predicted apogee in m.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The airframe is a stackup of: nose cone (ogive/tangent, ablative tip), upper body tube section housing the recovery bay, recovery bulkhead with shock-cord U-bolt, coupler tube joining upper and lower airframe sections, lower body tube section housing the motor mount, motor mount tube (inner tube) bonded to 3 centering rings, fin can region with 3 trapezoidal fins through-the-wall mounted to the motor mount tube, and a nozzle/retainer at the aft end. Structural load path at peak thrust: motor case -> motor retainer -> motor mount tube -> centering rings -> body tube -> coupler -> upper body tube -> recovery bulkhead -> shock cord. Load path at recovery shock: parachute -> shock cord -> U-bolt -> recovery bulkhead -> upper airframe. Fin side force path: fin -> fin root tabs -> motor mount tube (through-the-wall) + body tube fillet. The final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, stability/apogee/propellant caps and pass/fail thresholds from the original brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design a student sounding-rocket airframe as a multi-part assembly for the Korean student-rocket competition. GLOW 9 kg +/- 500 g; target apogee 400 m with 400 g solid propellant; static margin 1.0-2.5 cal; airframe 98 mm ID x 1.8 m length.

Load cases: LC1 launch thrust with 12 g axial; LC2 aerodynamic 8 g lateral; LC3 recovery 10 g parachute shock; LC4 fin-tip gust 75 N.

Pass/fail: no yield under any LC; axial buckling load factor >= 1.5. FEM at assembled level; interface DCR <= 1.0 at motor mount, bulkhead, and fin-can joints.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Stability caliber and apogee are computed from Barrowman / OpenRocket, not FEA.
Banned oxidizer list is a catalog lookup.

prompt.title: University-student rocket airframe (multi-part stackup assembly)
prompt.brief: Design a ~9 kg GLOW, 1.8 m long, 98 mm ID student rocket as a stacked assembly: nose cone, recovery bay in upper body tube, recovery bulkhead with U-bolt, coupler, lower body tube, motor mount tube on 3 centering rings, 3 trapezoidal through-the-wall fins, and nozzle/retainer. Apogee <= 400 m, propellant <= 400 g, stability 1.0-2.5 cal. Airframe survives 12 g axial + 8 g lateral + 10 g recovery shock + 75 N fin tip side load. Body tube buckling load factor >= 1.5 at 12 g. FEM verification at assembled level; connection DCR <= 1.0 on every epoxy-bonded and bolted interface.

material.nose_cone: molded fiberglass or 3D-printed PLA+ with ablative tip
material.bulkhead_and_centering_rings: 5 mm birch plywood or G10 FR4
material.motor_mount_tube: thin-wall phenolic, ID sized to solid motor casing
material.fins: 3 mm G10 FR4 or 4 mm plywood, through-the-wall tabs
material.fasteners_and_u_bolt: zinc-plated steel; epoxy (JB Weld / West Systems) for bonded joints

load_cases.name: motor_axial_12g
load_cases.description: peak thrust quasi-static; compresses body tube, drives buckling and bulkhead check

load_cases.name: lateral_gust_8g
load_cases.description: transverse gust on airframe side area; governs body tube bending and fin shear

load_cases.name: recovery_shock_10g
load_cases.description: parachute deployment jerk pulling on shock cord U-bolt and recovery bulkhead

load_cases.name: fin_tip_side_load
load_cases.description: static lateral force at fin tip; sizes fin root fillet and through-the-wall tab

boundary_conditions.motor_mount: fixed during burn (idealized clamp at motor retainer plane)
boundary_conditions.pre_launch_rail: rail buttons constrain Y/Z translation; axial free
boundary_conditions.recovery_deployment: shock cord tension reacted at U-bolt on recovery bulkhead

objective.design_heuristic: Keep motor impulse class and propellant mass such that OpenRocket predicted apogee is <= 395 m at the hottest expected day conditions, leaving a 5 m margin under the 400 m cap.

output_format.body_count: multi-body (one body per part family instance)

parts.name: nose_cone
parts.role: aerodynamic fairing at the forward tip; provides the first caliber of body-tube-referenced length and houses optional avionics on some configurations

geometric_constraints.profile: tangent ogive or Von Karman, length >= 3 calibers (>= 294 mm for 98 mm OD)

parts.material_hint: molded fiberglass laminate or 3D-printed PLA+ reinforced with epoxy-glass at shoulder

parts.name: upper_body_tube
parts.role: cylindrical airframe section housing the recovery system (parachute + shock cord + avionics bay) between the nose cone shoulder and the coupler

geometric_constraints.surface: external paintable; internal allowed for epoxy fillets

parts.material_hint: phenolic-impregnated paper tube or carbon-wrapped cardboard

parts.name: recovery_bulkhead
parts.role: circular disk bulkhead bonded inside the upper body tube that anchors the shock-cord U-bolt; transfers parachute deployment force into the airframe and caps the recovery bay
parts.count_note: Typically one forward bulkhead (nose-cone side) and one aft bulkhead (coupler side) define the recovery bay volume.
parts.material_hint: 5 mm birch plywood or G10 FR4, bonded with structural epoxy

parts.name: coupler
parts.role: shorter-diameter inner tube that slips into both the upper and lower body tube halves to join them at the recovery-bay aft end; carries all axial load between the two airframe sections
parts.material_hint: phenolic or G10 tube, bonded with structural epoxy on one side, shear-pinned or friction-fit on the other

parts.name: lower_body_tube
parts.role: cylindrical airframe section from the coupler to the aft end, housing the motor mount assembly and carrying the fin can

geometric_constraints.fin_slots: 3 longitudinal slots cut through the wall, equally spaced around circumference, sized to fin root tab + 0.5 mm clearance

parts.material_hint: phenolic-impregnated paper tube or carbon-wrapped cardboard

parts.name: motor_mount_tube_with_centering_rings
parts.role: inner thin-wall tube sized to the solid motor casing, bonded coaxially inside the lower body tube via 3 annular centering rings; transfers thrust from motor retainer forward into the airframe and carries the fin through-the-wall tabs
parts.count_note: One mount tube + 3 centering rings treated as one assembled sub-family; sum of rings + tube is 4 parts but indexed as one family instance with count_min=1.

geometric_constraints.fin_tab_contact: fins slot through body tube and bond to motor mount tube outer surface with epoxy fillet

parts.material_hint: phenolic tube + 5 mm plywood or G10 centering rings

parts.name: trapezoidal_fin
parts.role: planar aerodynamic stabilizer bonded through-the-wall to the motor mount tube and filleted to the outer body tube; sets static stability and takes lateral loads
parts.count_note: Rocket uses 3 equally-spaced trapezoidal fins (120 deg apart) per the conventional low-drag amateur configuration.
parts.material_hint: 3 mm G10 FR4 or 4 mm aircraft plywood

parts.name: nozzle_retainer_assembly
parts.role: aft-end fitting that retains the solid motor casing against thrust and closes the fin can; includes a threaded retainer ring and, on some designs, a convergent-only ceramic nozzle insert bonded to the motor grain

geometric_constraints.insert_material: ceramic or graphite if hot-case motor

parts.material_hint: machined 6061-T6 aluminum retainer ring; ceramic/graphite nozzle insert if KNSB

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Each bonded or mechanically fastened interface (IF1-IF7) must keep its demand-to-capacity ratio below unity under the governing load case. Governing cases: LC1 for coupler/centering-ring/motor-retainer bonds; LC3 for U-bolt-to-bulkhead and bulkhead-to-tube bond; LC4 for fin-to-motor-mount and fin-to-body-tube fillets.
Cumulative axial deflection of the full nose-to-nozzle stackup under 12 g must not exceed 3 mm; prevents coupler pull-out and keeps stability caliber stable under thrust.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell (tubes, nose cone, coupler) + solid (bulkheads, centering rings, fins, retainer)
analysis_types: linear static x 4 (LC1, LC2, LC3, LC4)
recommended_fea_setup.contact_modeling: tied / cohesive for epoxy bonds (coupler-to-tube, bulkhead-to-tube, centering-ring-to-tubes, fin-to-motor-mount-tube, nozzle retainer threads); bolt pretension for U-bolt at recovery bulkhead

interfaces.mediated_by: nose cone shoulder slip-fit into upper body tube, retained by shear pins (typ. 2x 2-56 nylon pins)
interfaces.load_path: aero drag + recovery ejection pressure from nose-cone base into upper body tube inner wall

interfaces.mediated_by: structural epoxy fillet bonding recovery bulkhead disk to upper body tube inner surface
interfaces.load_path: LC3 parachute deployment shock: U-bolt tension -> bulkhead -> tube wall in hoop shear

interfaces.mediated_by: epoxy bond on upper half of coupler; friction/shear-pin fit on lower half
interfaces.load_path: LC1 axial compression transmitted across the upper/lower airframe joint via coupler

interfaces.mediated_by: epoxy bond or shear pin (pair) with optional external wrap
interfaces.load_path: LC1 axial thrust load continues from lower body tube into coupler and then upper airframe

interfaces.mediated_by: epoxy fillet on 3 centering rings bonded to both the motor mount tube OD and the lower body tube ID
interfaces.load_path: LC1 thrust from motor mount tube into body tube via centering-ring shear

interfaces.mediated_by: through-the-wall fin tab epoxy-bonded to the motor mount tube OD plus external root fillet to body tube
interfaces.load_path: LC4 fin side load reacts directly into motor mount tube; LC2 lateral gust shared with body tube

interfaces.mediated_by: threaded motor retainer bearing on aft lip of motor mount tube; nozzle insert bonded to motor grain (not structural to airframe)
interfaces.load_path: LC1 full motor thrust reaction from retainer into motor mount tube

fem_check_policy.rationale: The rocket airframe is a stackup where load paths (thrust, recovery shock, fin side load) cross multiple bonded interfaces; isolated per-part checks cannot verify coupler pull-out, bulkhead anchoring, or fin root fillet behavior. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A 1.8 m amateur sounding rocket cannot physically be a single monolithic body: the motor is a consumable insert, the nose cone must separate for recovery, the coupler exists specifically to allow the airframe to split for ejection charge venting, and the fins are through-the-wall bonded into a dedicated fin can. The decomposition here is the canonical amateur rocketry stackup (Rouse-Tech / LOC Precision / BPS.space style).
notes.speculation_caveats: Exact part dimensions (coupler length, fin root chord, centering-ring thickness) are speculative until the team selects a motor class and recovery mass budget; the counts and topology are standard for this class of vehicle.

eval_coverage: multi-part stackup with bonded + mechanical interfaces
coupler pull-out and axial stackup deflection
